text.title: tailwind-design-tokens Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tailwind-design-tokens as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoded hex values in tailwind.config.js: colors: { primary: '#1A73E8' }
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hardcoded values require a code rebuild for any color change, cannot be overridden at runtime for theming, and are invisible to CSS variable consumers like Storybook or server-side rendering.

failure-mode.description: Using opacity modifier syntax (bg-primary/50) with CSS variable token values
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tailwind's opacity modifiers require the color value to be in RGB channels format, not a hex string or CSS variable reference. bg-primary/50 silently fails or produces incorrect output when --color-primary is a hex value.

failure-mode.description: Token naming mismatch between Figma Variables, Style Dictionary output, and Tailwind config
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Three different naming conventions (Figma uses /, Style Dictionary uses -, Tailwind uses .) cause drift that makes automated token pipelines fail and requires manual reconciliation on every design update.

failure-mode.description: Overwriting the full tailwind.config.js when regenerating tokens from a pipeline
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Full overwrites destroy manual customizations (custom screens, plugins, content paths). Always merge the generated token block into the existing config using Object.assign or spread, never replace.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: JIT cannot statically analyze string templates. The class bg-primary assembled at runtime will not exist in the generated CSS because JIT never saw the complete string bg-primary in a source file.

failure-mode.description: Generating a Tailwind v3 config for a Tailwind v4 project (or vice versa)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tailwind v4 uses a CSS-first configuration approach with @theme directive; there is no tailwind.config.js. Generating v3 config for a v4 project produces a file the framework ignores entirely.
